howItWorks.intro: This energy cost as % of household income calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Energy burden = annual energy cost / annual income × 100
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is energy poverty?
faq.answer: When a household spends more than 10% of income on energy, it's considered energy poverty. About 30% of US households experience high energy burden (>6%), with low-income households spending up to 20% on energy.
